speaker: Adam
text: Good morning, Moiz! Did you see the news about the drone strikes in Lebanon? At least eight people were killed, including two children.

speaker: Eve
text: Yeah, that's incredibly tragic. It’s such a stark reminder of the ongoing violence in that region. The strikes happened on a highway south of Beirut, right?

speaker: Adam
text: Exactly. It’s just heartbreaking. And in other global news, there’s been a series of tremors near Tehran, raising concerns over a major earthquake risk.

speaker: Eve
text: That’s wild. Iran is already in a sensitive spot with the geopolitical tensions, and now this? The Mosha fault is one of the most active seismic zones in the country.

speaker: Adam
text: Right, it really adds another layer of worry. Speaking of international affairs, a UN resolution has just called for freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. That’s a critical waterway for global oil trade.

speaker: Eve
text: The Strait of Hormuz? That's significant! I can see how tensions there could escalate quickly, especially with the ongoing conflicts in the region.

speaker: Adam
text: Definitely, and speaking of escalations, we have news from the ongoing Iran war as well. Trump is set to meet with Xi Jinping in Beijing, and analysts say he’s likely to make concessions regarding arms sales to Taiwan.

speaker: Eve
text: Oh, wow! That meeting could have huge implications, especially with tensions already high in the Gulf.

speaker: Adam
text: And on a different note, we just lost Jason Collins, the first openly gay active NBA player, after a battle with cancer. He was a significant figure in sports.

speaker: Eve
text: That's so sad. He came out publicly in 2013 and really paved the way for more openness in sports. His contributions went beyond basketball.

speaker: Adam
text: Absolutely. And speaking of sports, Messi has doubled his base salary to $28 million a year at Inter Miami. That’s more than twice what the second-highest-paid player in MLS makes!

speaker: Eve
text: Wow, talk about a pay gap! It’s amazing how much star power he brings to the league.

speaker: Adam
text: Moving over to tech news, we see Silicon Valley giants like Palantir and Google starting to turn into war contractors, selling AI-powered weapon systems.

speaker: Eve
text: That’s a big shift. It raises a lot of ethical questions about technology and warfare.

speaker: Adam
text: It really does. It’s a fascinating yet concerning trend. Now, let's take a look at the markets. The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF closed at $678.67, down 0.13%.

speaker: Eve
text: There’s no specific recommendation on that one, but there have been some interesting articles about its long-term growth potential.

speaker: Adam
text: Right, and NVIDIA Corporation closed at $220.78, up 0.61%. Analysts have a strong buy recommendation with a mean target price of $269.17.

speaker: Eve
text: Their stock seems to be riding high on the wave of AI demand. It’s interesting how AI is influencing the entire tech sector right now.

speaker: Adam
text: Speaking of which, Micron Technologies closed at $766.58, down 3.61%, but still holds a strong buy recommendation with a target mean of $573.90.

speaker: Eve
text: That’s quite a drop! But with so many analysts backing it, it sounds like they’re banking on a recovery.

speaker: Adam
text: Exactly. Alphabet Inc. closed at $387.35, down 0.33%, also with a strong buy recommendation and a target mean of $427.89.

speaker: Eve
text: They seem to be riding the coattails of AI developments too! It’s wild to see how intertwined these companies are.

speaker: Adam
text: Definitely. Meta Platforms saw a slight increase, closing at $603, up 0.69%, with a strong buy recommendation and a target mean of $826.69.

speaker: Eve
text: It’s interesting how the market responds to these tech giants. They really seem to be the backbone of the stock market right now.

speaker: Adam
text: ASML closed at $1520.94, down 2.87%, with a strong buy recommendation and a target mean of $1671.89.

speaker: Eve
text: And Taiwan Semiconductor closed at $397.28, down 1.79%. Analysts have a mean target of $463.45.

speaker: Adam
text: They’re facing some competitive pressure from AMD, which seems to be affecting the overall sentiment around chip stocks.

speaker: Eve
text: And finally, Cameco Corp. closed at $116.93, down 2.67%, with a buy recommendation and a target mean of $138.46.

speaker: Adam
text: There’s definitely some cautious optimism in that sector. Now, let’s wrap things up.

speaker: Eve
text: Sounds good! Thanks for tuning in, Moiz. Drive safe and have a great day!

speaker: Adam
text: Take care, Moiz!
